project_overview.project_name: Somalia Empowering Women through Education and Skills 'Rajo Kaaba' Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve literacy and numeracy skills of women in selected areas and to prepare women for leadership roles.
project_overview.total_financing: US$52.0 million (IDA Grant: US$25.0 million / SDR 18.6 million; Early Learning Partnership MDTF: US$2.0 million; Somalia Multi-Partner Fund: US$25.0 million)
project_overview.beneficiaries: At least 28,500 young women and up to 3,000 disadvantaged young men aged 15-35, primarily from marginalized and vulnerable populations including IDPs, across Somalia and Somaliland. At least 10% of beneficiaries to be persons with disabilities. Estimated 189,000 indirect family member beneficiaries.

primary_locations: Jubaland – Lower Jubba and Gedo (Kismayo, Luq, Dolow)
Puntland – Bari and Nugal (Baran, Eiyl, Buuhoodle)
Southwest – Bay and Lower Shabelle (Baidoa, Afgoye, Waajid)
Galmudug – Galgaduud and Mudug (Adado, Abudwak, Galkacyo)
Hirshabelle – Hiran and Middle Shabelle (Jalalaqsi, Warsheekh, Mahaday)
Somaliland – Maroodi-Jeex and Togdheer (Hargaisa, Burao, Erigavo)
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: Yes – Somalia is rated as a 'high-intensity' conflict country on the World Bank's fragile and conflict-affected situations list. The project covers all Federal Member States (Galmudug, Hirshabelle, Jubbaland, Puntland, South West), Banadir Administration, and Somaliland. Al-Shabaab militia activity poses ongoing security threats. Parts of the country are inaccessible due to security risks.

key_components.component_name: Component 1: Skills for Life and Labor Market Success
key_components.description: Supports adolescent girls and women to acquire basic literacy, numeracy, socioemotional/life skills, and basic vocational skills. Uses competitive, demand-driven process to hire Program Implementing Partners (PIPs) – NGOs, TVET centers, private companies – to deliver non-residential integrated education and skills training (up to 12 months). Includes stipends, childcare, community engagement, and follow-up employment support. Covers at least two districts per FMS and Somaliland. Includes climate mitigation/adaptation content and distribution of efficient cookstoves as completion bonuses.
key_components.financing: US$25.0 million (US$1M IDA + US$2M ELP MDTF + US$22M SMPF)

key_components.component_name: Component 2: Higher Skills Development for Women's Leadership
key_components.description: Establishes Women's Development Centers (WDCs) at 10-15 universities/HEIs through competitive grants (up to US$500,000 each) for mentorship, short courses, networking, and bridge programs. Also supports development of a consortium of autonomous women's colleges through feasibility study, inception report, design plans, legal establishment, and initial enrollment. Emphasizes digital skills, remote delivery, STEM, and leadership for political/public roles including the 30% parliamentary quota target.

key_components.component_name: Component 3: Technical Assistance and Project Management
key_components.description: Finances systems strengthening (EMIS modules for NFE, TVET, higher education), Skills Development Fund (SDF) development, technology-based M&E, impact evaluation, tracer studies, project management, capacity building for FGS/FMS/Somaliland staff, GBV prevention and response action plan implementation, and contracting of qualified GBV survivor services provider.

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Federal Republic of Somalia Ministry of Education, Culture and Higher Education (MoECHE) at federal level; FMS and Somaliland Ministries of Education for service delivery in respective jurisdictions.
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Steering Committee chaired by Federal Minister of MoECHE with FMS Ministers, ESC representative, Ministry of Labour, and MoF. Project Management Committee chaired by Director General MoECHE with FMS Director Generals. Shared PMU with Somalia EHCD Project (P172434). Separate Somaliland subsidiary agreement and operations manual. National Women's College Board for consortium governance. Technical selection committees for PIPs and WDC grants including civil society and private sector representatives. External Assistance Fiduciary Section (EAFS) within Offices of Accountant General at FGS, FMS, and Somaliland.
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations (July 2016, updated November 2020). All procurement treated as prior review by World Bank regardless of value. STEP tool used for all procurement transactions. PPSD and 18-month Procurement Plan prepared. Shared PMU procurement specialist from EHCD Project. Probity adviser to be engaged where appropriate. Procurement risk rated High.
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: PMU M&E officer coordinates across FMS/Somaliland PMUs, Higher Education Commission, and Women's College Board. Independent third-party verification agent validates PIP results before payments. Mobile technology (GEMS, SMS, tablets) for data collection in remote/conflict-affected areas. Beneficiary satisfaction surveys via mobile phone. EMIS modules for NFE, TVET, and higher education to be developed. Tracer studies for labor market outcomes. Impact evaluation of Component 1 second chance program. Annual reporting on beneficiary feedback with action plans.

identified_risks.risk_description: Issues related to women's empowerment not broadly endorsed within government or Somali society; risk of political changes post-election weakening strategic support for women's empowerment; disruption to FGS-FMS education policy dialogue during political transition; heightened insecurity and violence.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Upstream and continuous social engagement; broad stakeholder consultations including women activists, clan leaders, civil society, senior government officials; regular consultative group meetings during implementation; engagement beyond politically appointed leaders.

identified_risks.risk_description: Al-Shabaab militia and extremist-instigated violence make parts of the country inaccessible for project implementation and supervision. Security risks for project workers and students, especially at women's colleges. Possible backlash including GBV from conservative groups against women's education activities.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Pre-selecting geographic delivery zones less prone to political violence; continuous monitoring; mobile phone-based programs for at-home learning continuity; recruitment of certified security management firm (legal covenant within 3 months of effectiveness); Security Management Framework (SMF); area-specific Security Management Plans as disbursement condition; remote technology for resilience.

identified_risks.risk_category: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability
identified_risks.risk_description: Weak capacity of executive structures at all government levels; ambiguity in FGS-FMS roles; political interference in PIP selection; disruption of FGS-FMS collaboration; weak FM capacity by implementing agencies.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Semi-autonomous selection committee with non-governmental representatives; PMU operated by contracted experienced professionals; independent verification agent; capacity building activities from EHCD Project repurposed; MOU between MoECHE and Ministry of Labor clarifying roles.

identified_risks.risk_description: Weak institutional and system capacity at federal, state, and district levels; sustainability challenges as NFE sector largely donor-funded; lack of unified salary/payment policies; rudimentary EMIS; weak bidder capacity and limited service providers; high bid prices due to security conditions; fraud, corruption, and collusion; inadequate procurement planning experience.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: EAFS units within Offices of Accountant General; Use of Country Systems; all procurement as prior review; dedicated procurement specialist; World Bank FM supervision every 6 months; third-party contractors for targeted FM risks; POM with detailed FM procedures; STEP tool; probity adviser; technical assistance with knowledge transfer requirements.

identified_risks.risk_description: Construction/rehabilitation risks (noise, soil erosion, waste, occupational health, fire safety); under-resourced construction companies; COVID-19 exposure; security risks for workers and students; GBV/SEA/SH risks rated High; land acquisition and involuntary resettlement; labor influx; child labor; elite capture; human trafficking; exclusion of vulnerable groups; weak MoECHE environmental governance capacity.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: ESMF, LMP, SMF, SEP, ESCP disclosed May 2022; ESMP for construction phase as disbursement condition; SEA/SH Prevention and Response Action Plan; RAPs and land agreements as disbursement conditions; functioning GRM as disbursement condition; GBV specialist in PMU; codes of conduct; community consultations; safety mapping; referral pathways for GBV survivors.

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited government capacity to respond to external shocks; nascent formal economy; climate shocks impacting agricultural output and displacing people; COVID-19 and locust infestation recovery; global instability and rising fuel/food prices.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Grant proceeds finance almost all project activities reducing macroeconomic risk; collaboration with IMF on revenue mobilization; coordination with other World Bank projects and development partners.

identified_risks.risk_description: Negative reaction from community or religious leaders to girls' and women's enrollment in education; possible violence from extremist groups; diverse stakeholder objectives across FGS, FMS, and Somaliland.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Frequent direct stakeholder engagement; strong relationships encouraging open communication; engagement with community and religious leaders to build buy-in; public campaigns including social media to promote women's participation.

safeguards_and_social.environmental_category: High (Environmental and Social Risk Classification: High; Environmental risk: Substantial; Social risk: High)
triggered_safeguard_policies: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts (ESS1)
Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management (ESS3)
Land Acquisition, Restrictions on Land Use and Involuntary Resettlement (ESS5)
Biodiversity Conservation and Sustainable Management of Living Natural Resources (ESS6)
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes – Multi-channel GRM including radio, mobile phones, community meetings, email, websites, and in-person reports through community leaders. Toll-free mobile phone lines and email addresses at FMS and FGS levels. Social safeguards specialists receive, log, forward, and follow up grievances. Complainants may remain anonymous. Grievance committee requires representation of women and vulnerable groups. SEA/SH cases referred to dedicated GBV reporting protocols. GRM must be functioning and accessible as disbursement condition. World Bank GRS and Inspection Panel also available.
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Project is entirely gender-focused: targets adolescent girls and women (90% of beneficiaries); addresses GBV, FGM, early marriage, and social norms limiting women's education and economic participation; includes GBV prevention and response action plan; SEA/SH risk rated High with dedicated mitigation measures; codes of conduct for schools; safety mapping; female teacher support; awareness campaigns; engagement with community and religious leaders; women's quota support for 30% parliamentary representation; WDCs and women's colleges as safe spaces; mentorship and role model programs.
vulnerable_groups: Persons with disabilities (minimum 10% of beneficiaries; WG-SS used for identification)
Rural and nomadic women (83% never attended formal schooling)
Girls and women at risk of early marriage
Youth not in education, employment, or training (NEET)

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project explicitly designed for FCV context: draws on implementation lessons from FCV projects emphasizing simplicity and flexibility; uses both government and non-state actors for delivery; pre-selects geographic zones less prone to political violence; incorporates mobile/remote technology for continuity during security disruptions; certified security management firm required; area-specific Security Management Plans required before disbursement; third-party monitoring to reduce direct exposure; avoids duplication with other donor programs in conflict-affected areas; design recognizes risk of backlash against women's empowerment from extremist groups.
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Somalia rated 'high-intensity' conflict on World Bank FCV list. Per capita GDP US$480; 69% in poverty. Education system decimated during civil war; 80%+ of educated elite fled 1991-2008. Government expenditure-to-GDP ratio 12.4%; tax-to-GDP 2.5%. Al-Shabaab militia challenges government stability. Five Federal Member States with evolving federal-state relations. Somaliland declared independence 1991 (not internationally recognized) with relative stability. 56% of women illiterate vs 41% men; 57% of females 15-29 NEET. GBV widespread: 36% intimate partner violence, 62% FGM. Climate shocks (drought, floods, locusts) compound fragility. 4.8 million people in food crisis as of March 2022.
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Security risks are a key area of concern throughout project. Al-Shabaab and extremist groups may target women's education activities. World Bank security restrictions limit direct supervision; third-party contractors deployed for FM supervision. Legal covenant requires recruitment of certified security management firm within 3 months of effectiveness. Area-specific Security Management Plans required as disbursement condition for construction activities. Women's colleges require special security considerations for student safety. Mobile technology used to maintain service delivery continuity during security deterioration. Geographic targeting avoids most conflict-prone areas. Security costs reflected in high bid prices.
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: Yes – IDPs are explicitly identified as a key target beneficiary group. Approximately 83% of women in IDP settlements have never attended formal schooling. IDP settlements are among the most underserved populations. Project targets districts with IDP populations and includes IDPs in coverage criteria. Climate-induced displacement (drought, floods) also referenced as ongoing challenge affecting education access.
